summary: The session focused on the mental health and wellness resources available at the California Institute of Technology (Caltech), including counseling services, peer support programs, stress management resources, and specific offerings for graduate students. The user, a graduate student, inquired about the accessibility of these resources and the unique needs of graduate students.
facts: Caltech has a dedicated Counseling and Psychological Services (CAPS) that provides individual and group therapy to students, staff, and faculty. Students at Caltech can take a mental health leave of absence for up to one year to seek treatment and support. Caltech's Disability Services office offers individualized accommodations for students with mental health conditions under the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA). The Peer Health Advocates (PHA) program consists of trained student volunteers who promote health and wellness on campus. Caltech offers stress management workshops that teach techniques for managing stress, including mindfulness and relaxation strategies. Students can access meditation rooms on campus for quiet reflection and relaxation. The Caltech BeWell Program provides resources for stress management and healthy lifestyle choices. Graduate students at Caltech can participate in the Graduate Student Council (GSC) Mentoring Program for peer support and guidance. The Graduate Student Council hosts an annual retreat for graduate students during spring break to relax and connect with peers. Caltech's Counseling and Psychological Services (CAPS) offers therapy sessions specifically addressing the unique stressors faced by graduate students. The Graduate Life Office at Caltech provides resources and support services tailored to graduate students, including financial planning and career development support.
